overview.title: KV Botnet Targets Transportation Systems Through Vulnerable Devices
overview.summary: The KV Botnet campaign leverages a series of attacks to infiltrate transportation systems, starting with reconnaissance and ending with disruptive actions. The attack involves multiple stages, including exploitation of known vulnerabilities, persistence, privilege escalation, and data exfiltration.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: Adversaries use Shodan to scan for exposed transportation system devices, identifying potential targets for exploitation.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: Development of malware tailored to target SOHO devices commonly used in transportation systems.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 180 - Exploiting Incorrectly Configured Access Control Security Levels
stage.description: Exploits a known vulnerability in transportation devices to gain initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Executes malicious code on compromised devices to establish a foothold in the network.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Creates persistent registry keys to maintain access post-reboot.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Gains elevated privileges on infected devices to expand control.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Disables antivirus software to prevent detection and removal.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Extracts credentials for lateral movement within the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Maps the network to identify all connected devices and services.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Moves laterally across devices using stolen credentials to spread infection.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675 - Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Harvests sensitive data from transportation systems for potential misuse.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 277 - Data Interchange Protocol Manipulation
stage.description: Transfers collected data to a command-and-control server for further processing.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Causes operational disruption through DDoS or SCADA manipulation, leading to service outages.

Scenario.summary: The KV Botnet campaign systematically compromises transportation systems through a multi-stage attack, leveraging vulnerabilities, persistence, and lateral movement to achieve its objectives. The attack culminates in data exfiltration and service disruption, posing significant risks to critical infrastructure.
